notes: Published numbers are reference points from original papers on related but not identical splits/preprocessing. Runnable local implementations of GRAIN, Eckhoff GMN, and CrossAlert are simplified supervised upper bounds that require incident ground truth.

rule_based_lower_bound.description: Deterministic alert correlation without learned models.

weakly_supervised.description: Alert-level labels only; no incident-level supervision at training time.

flow_level_gnn.description: NetFlow-oriented GNN-IDS methods reimplemented on our alert graph.

supervised_upper_bound.description: Alert-correlation methods requiring incident or attack-step ground truth at training time.

domain_datasets.name: ait_ads
domain_datasets.citation: Landauer et al., CSET 2024 — AIT Alert Data Set

domain_datasets.name: darpa2000
domain_datasets.citation: DARPA 2000 LLDOS 1.0/2.0 Snort alert scenarios (five-phase multi-step attack)

domain_datasets.name: iscx2012
domain_datasets.citation: UNB ISCX IDS 2012 multi-step attack scenarios (day-3 alert correlation standard)

references.citation: Freitas & Gharib (2024). GraphWeaver: Scalable Alert Correlation With Dynamic Graphs. CIKM 2024.
references.metric_source: Industry rule-based entity-overlap correlation (Microsoft Defender XDR lineage).

references.citation: Xiao et al. (2025). GRAIN: Graph-based Alert Incident coNstruction. Computers & Security.
references.metric_source: Full GRAIN uses causality discovery + RL screening; local run is simplified causal-graph GNN with incident CE loss. Requires attack-step/incident GT.

references.citation: Eckhoff et al. (2025). Alert contextualisation with Graph Matching Networks.
references.metric_source: Full method matches alert groups to labeled historical incidents; local run uses incident prototype matching. Requires incident GT.

references.citation: Niknami et al. (2024). CrossAlert: Multi-stage network attack detection via alert analysis. IEEE CNS 2024.
references.metric_source: Local run fuses alert features, anomaly scores, and agglomerative clustering; uses incident GT when available.

references.citation: Sun et al. (2024). GNN-IDS: Graph Neural Network based Intrusion Detection System. ARES 2024.
references.metric_source: Reimplemented on our alert graph; original paper uses IDS-derived attack graphs.

references.citation: Guerra et al. (2025). Self-Supervised Learning of Graph Representations for Network Intrusion Detection. NeurIPS 2025.
references.metric_source: NF-CSE-CIC-IDS2018-v3 macro-F1 / PR-AUC (published NetFlow benchmark, not reproduced here).

references.citation: Caville et al. (2022). Anomal-E: A Self-Supervised Network Intrusion Detection System based on Graph Neural Networks. KBS.
references.metric_source: NF-UNSW-NB15 / NF-BoT-IoT (published NetFlow benchmark, not reproduced here).
